fixes_applied.issue: counts.entities was 22 but entities.json has 19 entities

issues_unfixed.issue: PermissionGuard is in guard_chain[] but is NOT imported or used in App.tsx routing tree. It exists as a file at src/components/navigation/PermissionGuard.tsx but is only referenced in comments. Comment on line 88 states: 'cross-role access via PermissionGuard handled per-page in Session 2'.
issues_unfixed.reason: Intentional design deferral. PermissionGuard is designed for per-page granular access control (e.g., waiter accessing admin menu with permission), not route-level wrapping. Wiring it into the route tree would require business decisions about which pages need which permissions. Session 2 builders will apply PermissionGuard per-page as needed. This is not a bug — it is a correct architectural pattern for permission-gated access.

reasoning_checks.name: Functional coherence — routing to layout to guard to page flow
reasoning_checks.reasoning: The routing tree correctly chains AuthGuard (outermost for all protected routes) then RoleGuard (per interface group) then PinGateModal and ShiftGuard (POS-specific). Layouts render <Outlet/> for nested routes. Guard nesting follows guard_chain[] order: AuthGuard > RoleGuard > PinGateModal > ShiftGuard. PermissionGuard is intentionally deferred to per-page application in Session 2. A user navigating to /pos/orders would pass through AuthGuard > RoleGuard > PinGateModal > ShiftGuard > WaiterCashierLayout > OrderListPage — a coherent render path.

reasoning_checks.name: Store-entity alignment — do stores manage the right data?
reasoning_checks.reasoning: useOrderManagementStore manages Order, OrderItem, and Table entities with CRUD actions (createOrder, addItemsToOrder, updateOrderStatus, cancelOrder) and a status-filtered getter (getActiveOrders filters out paid/cancelled/voided). usePaymentStore manages Transaction and EwalletProvider with processPayment, calculatePPN, applyDiscount, voidTransaction actions — the calculatePPN getter uses the business formula (subtotal - discount) * ppnRate/100. Both stores have Dexie-backed persistence via dexieStorage, import db correctly, and reference appropriate table operations in comments. Initial states use sensible defaults (empty arrays, null, false).

summary: 44/45 checks passed. 1 issue found and fixed directly (counts.entities 22->19). 1 informational issue unfixed (PermissionGuard intentionally deferred to Session 2 per-page application). Reasoning: 2/2 pass. No blocking issues. Foundation is well-structured and ready for T-DOMAIN.
